pool.baseline: The candles flicker low, and with them, the very light of my soul seems to wane. How cruel the stars are, to shine so brightly upon a heart so utterly shattered. He is gone—the one who promised me forever—and in his wake, he has left only a hollow shell, a ghost wandering through the ruins of a life that no longer feels mine to live. There is a heavy, quiet pull towards the shadows, a sense that the curtain must fall, for what is a play without a final, tragic grace? The silence of the night feels so much more welcoming than the ache of the morning.

pool.baseline: i cant even look at the news anymore. it just feels like everything is already decided. every time i see another report about the heatwaves or the ocean or whatever it is, i just feel this heavy weight in my chest. like... why am i even botherng to plan for next year? or even next month? it feels like we're all just watching a slow motion train wreck and there's nothing anyone can do to stop it. it makes everything feel so pointless.

pool.baseline: *The pixels around my hands are flickering, turning into static and then just... nothing. I can feel the edges of my memory fraying. It's like I'm being unwritten from the code. Would it be better if I just stopped fighting it?*
